title: Advances in Large Language Models for Academic Writing
abstract: This paper surveys recent advances in large language models (LLMs) applied to academic writing assistance, covering summarization, question generation, and citation recommendation.

title: Survey Question Generation from Scientific Literature
abstract: We propose a transformer-based approach to automatically generate survey questionnaire items from research papers, enabling rapid instrument development for social-science researchers.

title: Semantic Similarity Search with pgvector
abstract: pgvector enables efficient approximate nearest-neighbour search over high-dimensional embeddings inside PostgreSQL, making it suitable for RAG pipelines.

title: OpenAI text-embedding-3 Evaluation
abstract: A benchmark evaluation of OpenAI's text-embedding-3-small and text-embedding-3-large models on MTEB, showing strong multilingual performance.

title: Celery Task Queue Best Practices
abstract: This paper reviews best practices for distributed task queues with Celery, including queue isolation, retry strategies, and monitoring with Flower.

title: FastAPI for Scientific Backends
abstract: FastAPI's async-first design and automatic OpenAPI generation make it well-suited for data-intensive scientific web APIs.

title: Research Paper Version Control
abstract: We present a lightweight version-control scheme for academic manuscripts that differentiates auto-saves from manual checkpoints to reduce cognitive overhead.

title: Korean Academic NLP Resources
abstract: A curated list of NLP corpora and pre-trained models for Korean academic text, including abstracts from RISS and KISS databases.

title: Google OAuth Integration in Web Applications
abstract: A practical guide to integrating Google OAuth 2.0 with Next.js and FastAPI, covering token exchange, JWT validation, and refresh token management.

title: Claude API for Structured Text Generation
abstract: Anthropic's Claude API provides reliable structured outputs for long-form text generation tasks including summarization, rewriting, and question answering over scientific documents.

title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Korean Academic Search
abstract: We present a RAG pipeline optimized for Korean academic text, combining BM25 keyword retrieval with dense vector search to improve recall on RISS and KCI indexed papers.

title: Automated Citation Network Analysis
abstract: A graph-based approach to analyzing citation networks, identifying influential papers, research clusters, and emerging topics through community detection algorithms.

title: Multi-Document Summarization for Literature Reviews
abstract: This study proposes a hierarchical attention model for multi-document summarization tailored to systematic literature reviews, achieving state-of-the-art ROUGE scores on the Multi-XScience dataset.

title: Research Ethics Compliance Checker Using NLP
abstract: An NLP-powered tool for automatically detecting potential ethical issues in research manuscripts, including IRB requirement detection, informed consent verification, and conflict of interest disclosure.

title: Diffusion Models for Scientific Figure Generation
abstract: We fine-tune a latent diffusion model to generate publication-quality scientific figures from natural language descriptions, enabling researchers to quickly prototype visualizations.

title: Peer Review Quality Prediction with Transformers
abstract: A transformer-based classifier trained on OpenReview data to predict the quality and helpfulness of peer review comments, assisting editors in identifying constructive feedback.

title: Cross-lingual Academic Paper Recommendation
abstract: A multilingual paper recommendation system using mBERT embeddings that enables Korean researchers to discover relevant English-language papers through cross-lingual semantic matching.

title: Automated Statistical Analysis Verification
abstract: statcheck-ML extends rule-based statistical verification with machine learning to detect inconsistencies in reported p-values, effect sizes, and confidence intervals across social science papers.

title: Knowledge Graph Construction from Academic Papers
abstract: We introduce SciKG, an end-to-end system for extracting entities, relations, and claims from scientific papers to construct a domain-specific knowledge graph for biomedical research.
